Field.Name: Longitude
Field.Description: MUST be interpreted according to the CoordinateReferenceSystem

Field.Name: Latitude
Field.Description: MUST be interpreted according to the CoordinateReferenceSystem

Field.Name: Elevation
Field.Description: MUST be interpreted according to the ElevationReference. If floor, the height relative to specific floor in meter

Field.Name: Accuracy
Field.Description: The horizontal accuracy of the position in meters.

Field.Name: Floor
Field.Description: A logical and non-localized representation for a building floor. Floor 0 represents the floor designated as 'ground'. Negative numbers indicate floors below the ground floor and positive numbers indicate floors above the ground floor. When implemented, the floor value MUST match described logical numbering scheme, which can be different from any numbering used within a building. Values can be expressed as an integer value, or as a float as required for mezzanine floor levels.

Field.Name: Orientation
Field.Description: Informs about an orientation typically with respect to a position. In mathematics, orientation defines a geometric notion.

UADataType.Description: Defines a pair of coordinates - local and global - to allow geo-references from local coordinate to a global coordinate system

Field.Name: GlobalPosition
Field.Description: Position in a global coordinate reference system

Field.Name: LocalPosition
Field.Description: Local position in metric coordinates (in meter)

UAVariable.Description: Collects the data type descriptions of http://opcfoundation.org/UA/GPOS/
Indicates that all of the DataType definitions represented by the DataTypeDictionaryType are available through a DataTypeDefinition Attribute.
Collects the data type descriptions of http://opcfoundation.org/UA/GPOS/
Indicates that all of the DataType definitions represented by the DataTypeDictionaryType are available through a DataTypeDefinition Attribute.

UAVariableType.Description: Describes a point (i.e. a position) in 2 or 3 dimensions. Important: A Point object shall be interpreted according to the CoordinateReferenceSystem.

UAVariable.Description: The horizontal accuracy of the position in meters.

A projection identifier defining the projection of the provided location coordinate. The CoordinateReferenceSystem shall be either a valid EPSG identifier (https://epsg.io) or 'local' if the location is provided as a relative coordinate of the floor plan. For best interoperability and worldwide coverage, WGS84 (EPSG:4326) should be the preferred projection (as used also by GPS).
MUST be interpreted according to the ElevationReference. If floor, the height relative to specific floor in meter

An elevation reference hint for the position's Elevation. If present it shall be either 'floor' or 'wgs84'. If set to 'floor' the Elevation shall be assumed to be relative to the floor level in meter. If set to 'wgs84' the Elevation shall be treated as WGS84 ellipsoidal height. For the majority of applications an accurate geographic height may not be available. Therefore ElevationReference shall be assumed 'floor' by default if it is not present.

A logical and non-localized representation for a building floor. Floor 0 represents the floor designated as 'ground'. Negative numbers indicate floors below the ground floor and positive numbers indicate floors above the ground floor. When implemented, the floor value MUST match described logical numbering scheme, which can be different from any numbering used within a building. Values can be expressed as an integer value, or as a float as required for mezzanine floor levels.
MUST be interpreted according to the CoordinateReferenceSystem
MUST be interpreted according to the CoordinateReferenceSystem
Reference to the zone or provider calculating the position

UAVariableType.Description: Defines the location of an object - in a global coordinate reference system

UAVariable.Description: Informs about an orientation typically with respect to a position. In mathematics, orientation defines a geometric notion.

A projection identifier defining the projection of the provided location coordinate. The CoordinateReferenceSystem shall be either a valid EPSG identifier (https://epsg.io) or 'local' if the location is provided as a relative coordinate of the floor plan. For best interoperability and worldwide coverage, WGS84 (EPSG:4326) should be the preferred projection (as used also by GPS).
MUST be interpreted according to the CoordinateReferenceSystem
MUST be interpreted according to the CoordinateReferenceSystem
Reference to the zone or provider calculating the position

UAObjectType.Description: Defines an area, in which a local location is computed, e.g. via a RTLS or an proximity based system like RFID. For that reason it might have a set of GroundControlPoints defining the extent or just a point and a radius.

UAVariable.Description: Name of the building
Name of the floor
Points defining the extent of the zone
Indicates if the configuration is complete. FALSE if configuration is complete.
Describes a point (i.e. a position) in 2 or 3 dimensions.
For proximity-based systems. Describes the zone as a circular region with a given radius in meters around the position.
Name of the site
A unique identifier of the zone (e.g. a GUID).
